title: All the containers share the same ports
body:
Hello,

We have 3 containers like in our bellow example. each container has his own port. But, after applying the kube file, all the containers share the same ports, is this the correct and expected behavior?

```apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  labels:
    app: aaaa
  name: aaaa
spec:
  containers:
    - image: zzzz
      name: zzzz
      args:
        - '-f'
        - '/opt/mongod.conf'
      ports:
        - name: mongoport
          hostPort: 27017
          containerPort: 27017
          protocol: tcp
      env:
        - name: MONGO_INTERNAL_PORT
          value: "27017"
		  
    - image: yyyy
      name: yyyy
      ports:
        - name: fport
          hostPort: 8081
          containerPort: 8081
          protocol: tcp
      env:
        - name: ACCEPT
          value: "yes"

    - image: xxxx
      name: xxxx
      ports:
        - name: tport
          hostPort: 8899
          containerPort: 8899
          protocol: tcp
      env:
        - name: ACCEPT
          value: "yes" ```

```439f8ffc3dd6  xxx                    f /opt/mongod.co...  2 hours ago   Up 2 hours ago   0.0.0.0:8081->8081/tcp, 0.0.0.0:8899->8899/tcp, 0.0.0.0:27017->27017/tcp  mongodb
780aaa67f81b  yyy                                         2 hours ago   Up 2 hours ago   0.0.0.0:8081->8081/tcp, 0.0.0.0:8899->8899/tcp, 0.0.0.0:27017->27017/tcp  xxx_pod
10bd47cdab5e  zzz                   npm run cli           2 hours ago   Up 2 hours ago   0.0.0.0:8081->8081/tcp, 0.0.0.0:8899->8899/tcp, 0.0.0.0:27017->27017/tcp  zzz-pod
```

comments.body: Yes this is correct, a pod shares the network namespace by default. Therefore all containers have access to the same ports.
